title: test-helpers-shared-module: dedupe parse_id_from_stdout across tests/
body:
# Goal

Move `parse_id_from_stdout` (and any other test helpers that
appear in more than one place) into a shared module under
`crates/jjf/tests/common/mod.rs`, eliminating the current
3-way duplication.

# Source

Final whole-branch review of the `--parent` feature (2026-06-28
drop-epic-label) flagged this as Minor #4. `parse_id_from_stdout`
is defined three times:

- `crates/jjf/tests/ls.rs:~154` (returns String)
- `crates/jjf/tests/ready.rs:~145` (returns IssueId)
- `crates/jjf/tests/search.rs:~135` (returns String)

Same shape, three places. Tests are compiled per-file in Cargo's
integration-test layout, so a shared helper needs a
`tests/common/mod.rs` (or similar) that each test file pulls in
via `mod common;`.

# Approach

- Audit `crates/jjf/tests/*.rs` for repeated helpers — there are
  likely more candidates besides `parse_id_from_stdout`
  (`make_initialized_repo`, `run_jjf`, `run_jjf_with_stdin`,
  `bookmark_tip_commit`).
- For each helper that appears in 2+ files, move to
  `crates/jjf/tests/common/mod.rs`.
- Pick ONE return shape for `parse_id_from_stdout` — likely
  `IssueId` (richer; lets a future test assert on `.as_str()`
  or pass directly to `Storage::read`).
- Each test file gains `mod common;` and `use common::*;`.
- `crates/jjf/tests/common/mod.rs` does NOT get its own
  `[[test]]` (the file naming `common` is conventional but
  Cargo treats anything under `tests/` as a test binary unless
  it's in a subdirectory — verify with `cargo build --tests`).

# Acceptance

- [ ] Single definition of each previously-duplicated helper.
- [ ] All ls/ready/search/etc. tests still pass.
- [ ] No `cargo build --tests` warnings about the common module
      being treated as its own integration test binary.
- [ ] New test additions reach for the shared helpers, not new
      copy-paste.
